reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff date (May 15, 2025), President Trump and his administration are in the midst of intensive negotiations with Iran, having completed four rounds of talks in Oman and Italy. On May 15, Trump publicly stated that the US is 'getting very close' to a deal and that Iran has 'sort of' agreed to the terms. Additionally, reports from April 2025 indicated that Trump set a two-month deadline for these negotiations, which would expire in mid-June 2025. Market signals (falling oil prices) and Israeli media reports (stating a 'high chance' of a deal) further support the likelihood of an imminent announcement. While significant disagreements remain regarding Iran's uranium enrichment, the high-level momentum and the proximity of the reported deadline suggest a high probability that some form of agreement—at least an interim or framework deal—will be signed or announced before July 1, 2025.
